title: Auto-advance questions and display in continuous vertical layout

requirements.summary: Remove the Next button from the test interface so students no longer need to press it to move to the next question
acceptanceCriteria: Next button is not visible during test-taking
Students can still navigate through questions without clicking Next

file_operations.description: Remove the Next button from the navigation controls. Since all questions will be displayed continuously in a vertical layout, the Next button is no longer needed for question navigation. Keep the Submit button and timer/fullscreen controls intact.

requirements.summary: Display all test questions in a continuous vertical row, one after another, instead of showing one question at a time
acceptanceCriteria: All questions appear vertically stacked on the same page
Students can scroll through all questions without pagination
Answer inputs for all questions are visible simultaneously

file_operations.description: Refactor the test interface to render all questions in a continuous vertical list instead of displaying one question at a time. Map over all questions in the test and render each TestQuestionDisplay component sequentially. Ensure each question's answer input is bound to the corresponding answer state. Remove currentQuestionIndex-based conditional rendering.

Add a question number display at the top of each question component to help students identify which question they are viewing in the continuous vertical layout. Ensure the component accepts and displays its position in the question list.

requirements.summary: Maintain the reading test two-panel layout with passage on the left and all questions displayed vertically on the right panel
acceptanceCriteria: Reading tests continue to show passage in left panel
All reading test questions appear in continuous vertical scroll in right panel

file_operations.description: Update the ReadingTestLayout to render all questions in a continuous vertical list in the right panel instead of showing one question at a time. Map over all questions and render each TestQuestionDisplay component with its corresponding answer state. Keep the passage display in the left panel unchanged. Ensure both panels remain independently scrollable.

requirements.summary: Update progress tracking and navigation to work with the continuous question display instead of current question index
acceptanceCriteria: Progress indicator reflects which questions have been answered
Submit button remains accessible after all questions
Timer and fullscreen controls continue to function correctly

file_operations.description: Update the progress tracking logic to calculate and display the number of answered questions out of total questions instead of relying on currentQuestionIndex. Replace question navigation state management with answer tracking logic. Ensure the Submit button is positioned at the bottom of the question list and remains visible. Keep timer and fullscreen controls in their current positions and ensure they continue to function correctly with the new layout.
